decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 425.25 to ~637.8), which is within the allowed 2.0x step limit and stays under the 500.0 bound is not possible, so I must cap at the bound or adjust. Wait, the bound is 500.0. Current is 425.25. Max allowed is 500.0. So I will set it to 500.0.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity to its upper bound maximizes lateral heat spreading, directly reducing the localized peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the material upper bound
If this single change is insufficient, geometric changes (spreader width/height) will be required in the next step
